title: Income Inequality in the United States, 1913-1998
The Dynamic Effects of Personal and Corporate Income Tax Changes in the United States
Why Do Developing Countries Tax So Little?
Growth in a Time of Debt
How Progressive is the U.S. Federal Tax System?
Optimal Taxation and Public Production I: Production Efficiency
authors: Peter A. Diamond, James A. Mirrlees
methodology_summary: Theoretical welfare economics framework deriving conditions for optimal commodity taxation and public production. Proves production efficiency theorem: optimal tax system should not distort production even when lump-sum taxes are unavailable.

title: An Exploration in the Theory of Optimum Income Taxation
methodology_summary: Optimal control theory applied to income taxation. Derives nonlinear income tax schedule maximizing social welfare subject to incentive compatibility and revenue constraints under unobservable productivity heterogeneity.

title: The Elasticity of Taxable Income with Respect to Marginal Tax Rates: A Critical Review
authors: Emmanuel Saez, Joel Slemrod, Seth H. Giertz
methodology_summary: Comprehensive literature review and meta-analysis of ETI estimates. Surveys identification strategies, distinguishes real supply-side responses from avoidance, and summarizes elasticity ranges. Central estimate: ETI ≈ 0.12–0.40 for broad income base.

title: Optimal Taxation in Theory and Practice
authors: N. Gregory Mankiw, Matthew Weinzierl, Danny Yagan
methodology_summary: Reviews optimal tax theory literature and compares theoretical prescriptions to actual tax policy. Identifies six lessons from theory: flat consumption taxes, no capital income taxes, highly progressive income taxes under some welfarism, taxation of negative externalities, equal treatment of equal earners. Discusses gaps between theory and practice.

title: OECD Revenue Statistics 2023
methodology_summary: Annual compilation of tax revenue statistics for OECD member countries. Covers tax revenue as % of GDP by tax type (income, payroll, corporate, VAT, property, other), government expenditure, fiscal balance, and public debt. Based on national accounts and OECD standardized classification. Covers 38 OECD countries 1965-present. Ingested: 29 OECD countries, 2015-2022 for core taxation constructs.

title: The Macroeconomic Effects of Tax Changes: Estimates Based on a New Measure of Fiscal Shocks
authors: Christina D. Romer, David H. Romer
methodology_summary: Narrative identification of exogenous tax changes from presidential/congressional records 1945-2007. Distributed lag OLS estimates of tax change effects on output.

title: Unwilling or Unable to Cheat? Evidence from a Tax Audit Experiment in Denmark
authors: Henrik Jacobsen Kleven, Martin B. Knudsen, Claus Thustrup Kreiner, Soren Pedersen, Emmanuel Saez
methodology_summary: RCT: 40,000 Danish taxpayers randomly assigned to audit vs. control. DID identifies causal effect of audit threat on reported income by income source type.

title: Determinants of Revenue Performance
authors: Sanjeev Gupta, Benedict Clements, Alexander Pivovarsky, Erwin R. Tiongson
methodology_summary: Cross-country panel regression and stochastic frontier analysis of tax/GDP determinants. Derives tax effort index. 70+ countries 1975-2000.

title: No Taxation Without Information: Deterrence and Self-Enforcement in the Value Added Tax
methodology_summary: Randomized audit threat RCT with Chilean VAT firms. Shows upstream audit threats cause downstream compliance via invoice paper trail self-enforcement.

title: Do Taxpayers Bunch at Kink Points?
methodology_summary: Bunching estimator on IRS Statistics of Income data. Identifies ETI from excess mass at tax schedule kink points. Compares wage earners vs. self-employed.

title: Taxing across Borders: Tracking Personal Wealth and Corporate Profits
methodology_summary: Estimates offshore wealth and corporate profit shifting using IIP discrepancies and BEA foreign affiliate statistics. Finds 8% of global household wealth offshore, US MNEs book 20% of profits in havens.

title: The Missing Profits of Nations
authors: Thomas R. Torslov, Ludvig S. Wier, Gabriel Zucman
methodology_summary: National accounts and FATS data to estimate ~40% of global MNE profits shifted to tax havens annually. Data available at missingprofits.world.
